content.title: 🌙 Ayyam al-Bid Fasting Begins on June 29
content.subtitle: Three days of fasting, immense reward
content.body:
Bangladesh's Ayyam al-Bid fasts for the month of Muharram will fall on June 29, June 30, and July 1 (Monday, Tuesday, and Wednesday). Those who intend to observe these voluntary fasts should have Suhoor on Sunday night and begin fasting on Monday.

The term "Ayyam al-Bid" consists of two Arabic words: Ayyam (days) and Bid (white or luminous). It refers to the 13th, 14th, and 15th days of every lunar month. Several authentic hadiths encourage Muslims to fast on these three days. The Prophet ﷺ regularly observed these fasts throughout his life.

Fasting three days every month is considered equivalent in reward to fasting throughout the year. Therefore, let us prepare ourselves and encourage our families to take part in this beautiful Sunnah. Fasting is a unique act of worship for which Allah Himself has promised a special reward.

May Allah grant us the ability to observe these blessed voluntary fasts consistently. Ameen.
